context.projectOverview: Phase 6 — The Medical Team. A local-first, conversational health/wellness Team instance running a fixed SOP (consent -> red-flag-gate -> numerics -> retrieve -> answer-with-abstention). Regulatory refusals, the emergency short-circuit, numerics, and zero-egress are CODE-ENFORCED, never prompt-only. This sprint (S2) adds the first code-enforced gate (consent) + the audit substrate.

completedSprints.title: Additive pipeline plumbing + medical team registration
completedSprints.summary: commit 60215d2 — added 'medical-sop' to PipelineEngineName + both mirrored Zod enums; extended both exhaustive switches in src/orchestrator/workflow/selector.ts; created src/medical/{types.ts, engine.ts (MedicalSopEngine stub, zero LLM calls), team.ts (buildMedicalTeam + buildMedicalGuardrails)}; registered built-in 'medical' team in src/teams/registry.ts. Byte-zero impact on ts|skill|workflow + programming team.

criticalConstraints: MedicalSopEngine MUST keep a zero-arg constructor — src/orchestrator/workflow/selector.ts:126 calls `new MedicalSopEngine()` with no args. Inject fakes (clock/audit/consent) via an OPTIONAL deps object with safe real defaults, never required constructor params.

Fail-closed: with no recorded consent, MedicalSopEngine.run returns a refuse verdict and makes ZERO downstream calls (no numerics, no retrieval, no LLM). Verify with a spy LLMClient asserted never invoked.

AuditLog entries hold IDs/enums ONLY (event, rulesetVersion, patternsetVersion, ruleId) — NEVER prompt text or health values. File opened O_APPEND|O_CREAT, mode 0600. Reuse the append-only mode-0600 JSONL pattern from src/incident/timeline.ts:67-82.

ALL timestamps (consent acceptedAtIso, audit tIso) are INJECTED parameters (e.g. a `now: string` arg or injected clock), NEVER read from the system wall clock. Reuse the injected-timestamp pattern from src/state/memory.ts:66-72. A deterministic test must assert this.

ESM/NodeNext with .js import extensions; strict TS; no SDK types outside src/providers/; no sync fs except the (future) better-sqlite3 store — consent/audit/disclaimer use node:fs/promises or the explicit-mode append pattern. Tests use temp dirs + cleanup, NO fs mocks.

Do NOT implement the red-flag detector logic (Sprint 3), HealthDataStore/numerics (Sprint 4), ingestion (Sprint 5), egress/full SOP (Sprint 6), or retrieval (Sprint 7). This sprint is consent gate + audit log + disclaimer + wiring Gate 1 ONLY.
